data.title: In the past hour, the entire network has liquidated 134 million US dollars, mainly short orders
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Coinglass data shows that in the past hour, the entire network has been liquidated for $134 million, of which long orders have been liquidated for $35.6778 million and short orders have been liquidated for $98.5316 million.

data.title: Fed's Kugler: Anti-inflation process may have stalled
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Fed's Kugler said the latest data showed that progress toward the 2% inflation target may have stalled. In view of stable economic activity and employment, as long as the upside risks to inflation persist, it supports maintaining the current policy interest rate unchanged. Inflation expectations are rising, and there are upside risks to the upcoming policy changes (to inflation). It is gratifying that the increase in long-term inflation expectations has been small so far. Given the recent high inflation, consumer expectations may be more sensitive to further price increases. Labor market indicators show continued slowdown, but it will not weaken significantly. (Jinshi)

data.title: Trump: A major bill in Congress will include tax cuts
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: US President Trump: An important bill in Congress will include tax cuts. (Jinshi)

data.title: Trump: 20% retaliatory tariffs will be imposed on EU countries one by one, and 24% tariffs will be imposed on Japanese goods
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Trump said that the United States will impose 20% retaliatory tariffs on the European Union country by country, and will impose a 24% tariff on goods imported from Japan. (Jinshi)

data.title: The specific tariff rates of the United States on multiple countries are released
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: US President Trump said that Vietnam will be subject to a 46% tariff. The UK will be subject to a 10% tariff. Switzerland will be subject to a 31% tariff. Cambodia will be subject to a 49% tariff. Thailand will be subject to a 36% tariff. South Africa will be subject to a 30% tariff. Indonesia will be subject to a 32% tariff. Brazil will be subject to a 10% tariff. Singapore will be subject to a 10% tariff. South Korea will be subject to a 25% tariff. (Jinshi)

data.title: Affected by Trump's announcement of tariffs, BTC briefly hit 88,500 USDT before falling back
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News OKX market data shows that BTC briefly touched 88,500 USDT and then fell back, now reporting 85,333 USDT, a 24-hour increase of 0.33%. Earlier news, Trump announced the specific tariff rates of the United States on many countries.

data.title: Trump's Tariff Announcement Boosts Risk Assets and Bitcoin
data.description: According to Odaily, U.S. President Donald Trump announced a comprehensive tariff rate of 10%, which was lower than market expectations. This led to a surge in risk assets, with Nasdaq futures rising over 2% in after-hours trading. The U.S. dollar index (DXY) experienced a short-term drop of 50 points, while Bitcoin saw a rapid increase of over $1,500. Spot gold initially fell but later rebounded.

data.title: BTC briefly fell below 86,000 USDT, with a 24-hour drop of 1.21%
data.description: OKX market shows that BTC briefly fell below 86,000 USDT and is now at 85,977.8 USDT, a 24-hour drop of 1.21%. (This newsletter is generated with the assistance of AI)

data.title: Trump's tariff rate is lower than expected, risk assets rise sharply
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Trump announced a 10% tariff rate, which was lower than market expectations. Risk assets strengthened across the board. Nasdaq futures rose by more than 2% after hours. The US dollar index DXY fell 50 points in the short term, and Bitcoin rose by more than $1,500 in the short term. Spot gold fell first and then rose. (Jinshi)

data.title: West Virginia's Bitcoin Reserve Bill Aims to Enhance State Sovereignty
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, West Virginia's proposed Bitcoin strategic reserve bill seeks to bolster state sovereignty and provide freedom from a potential central bank digital currency (CBDC). State Senator Chris Rose, the bill's sponsor, emphasized the importance of decentralized currency and the public's desire for financial independence from federal control. The bill, introduced in February, proposes allowing the state treasury to invest up to 10% of public funds in precious metals, stablecoins, or digital assets with a market capitalization exceeding $750 million over the past year, with Bitcoin currently being the sole qualifying asset.

Senator Rose explained that the market cap requirement was designed to ensure the state's exposure to cryptocurrency while avoiding investments in volatile assets like memecoins. He believes adopting Bitcoin at the state level would enhance state sovereignty and align with the interests of individuals who already invest in Bitcoin privately. The proposed 10% allocation of state funds is intended to introduce Bitcoin to the state in a manner that minimizes fear among those unfamiliar with digital assets, providing a balanced exposure.

Despite the potential benefits, the bill faces challenges due to skepticism among lawmakers and financial experts regarding Bitcoin's volatility and price fluctuations. Concerns about financial instability make Bitcoin a contentious choice for state investments. While similar Bitcoin strategic reserve bills have emerged in various state legislatures across the United States, many have struggled to pass or have been modified significantly, even in conservative states. Currently, 47 such bills have been introduced in 26 states, with notable progress in Arizona, Oklahoma, and Texas.

Senator Rose clarified that the funds allocated to precious metals, stablecoins, or Bitcoin would be sourced from the state's pensions fund and severance tax fund, allowing divestment from existing ETF funds. This approach aims to keep the investments separate from the petty cash fund used for daily state expenses, focusing instead on longer-term assets. West Virginia Governor Patrick Morrisey, who envisions a future state economy driven by crypto and technology, is not expected to oppose the bill, nor is the state treasurer, who was consulted prior to its introduction.
